response:
Sec. 1  
In 2019, the Chief Minister of Andhra Pradesh was Y. S. Jaganmohan Reddy. He was the leader of the YSRCP party. His tenure started in 2014 after the state's formation.  

***  
Y. S. Jaganmohan Reddy was born in 1970 in Kadapa district. He became the youngest Chief Minister of Andhra Pradesh at the age of 42. His leadership style focused on development and youth empowerment.  

***  
He implemented various welfare schemes for the people of Andhra Pradesh. His government focused on infrastructure and education reforms. Y. S. Jaganmohan Reddy is known for his strong political presence.  

***  
The YSRCP party won the 2019 state elections with a significant majority. Y. S. Jaganmohan Reddy continued his leadership in the new government. His policies aimed at improving the quality of life for citizens.  

***  
He is also known for his efforts in promoting digital literacy and e-governance. Y. S. Jaganmohan Reddy has been a prominent figure in the Telugu political landscape. His leadership has shaped the state's political direction.  

***  
The Chief Minister has been involved in various social initiatives and public welfare programs. Y. S. Jaganmohan Reddy's government has focused on inclusive growth. His leadership has been marked by both achievements and challenges.  

***  
He has faced criticism for some of his policies and decisions. Y. S. Jaganmohan Reddy continues to be a central figure in Andhra Pradesh politics. His tenure has had a lasting impact on the state's development.  

***  
Y. S. Jaganmohan Reddy remains the Chief Minister of Andhra Pradesh as of 2023. His leadership has been a defining feature of the state's political scene. His policies and governance continue to shape the state's future.
